title: File not writable error in Docker setup
symptom: When attempting to write to a file in a shared folder, n8n throws an error stating that the file is not writable.
applies_when: Trying to write to a shared folder
root_cause: The error occurs because n8n restricts file access for security reasons. Without proper configuration, n8n cannot write to the specified directory in the Docker container, leading to this error.
fix_steps: Add the environment variable 'N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO=/data/shared' under the n8n service
agent_instruction: Add the N8N_RESTRICT_FILE_ACCESS_TO environment variable to your Docker Compose file and restart the container.
